revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Funakiyama Kofun cluster]][[ja:船来山古墳群]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofuns in Motosu, Chūbu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Funakiyama Kofun group | native_name =船来山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Funakiyama Kofun No.62, Motosu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Funakiyama Kofun No.62 | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Funakiyama Kofun group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|30|N|136|40|59|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Motosu, Gifu|en|Motosu, Gifu|qid=Q844047|lt=Motosu, Gifu|1=Motosu, Gifu}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Gifu|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}}; {{ill|Motosu|qid=Q844047|1=Motosu, Gifu}}| region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 155,420.74 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Funakiyama Kofun group'''|船来山古墳群|Funakiyama kofun-gun}} is a cluster of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located the city of {{ill|Motosu, Gifu|en|Motosu, Gifu|qid=Q844047|lt=Motosu|1=Motosu, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2018.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00004060|title=船来山古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Funaiyama Kofun group is the largest cluster of ''kofun'' in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}}, and was constructed from the 3rd to the 7th century, on a mountain called Mount Funaki stra11ling the border of the cities of Motosu and {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|qid=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}}. To date, 290 Kofuns have been discovered. Mount Funaki is a long and narrow mountain about 2 kilometers east-to-west by about 600 meters north-to-south, with an elevation of 64 to 115 meters. The ancient Kofuns are built from the foot of the mountain to the summit, and consist of various types of ''kofun'': {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (''hōfun'' (方墳)), {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (''empun'' (円墳)), two "cojoined-rectangle" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) and keyhole-style (''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=zempō-kōen fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}'' (前方後円墳)). Approximately a quarter of the mountain is covered by the National Historic Site designation, including 111 tumuli (112 if a single {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} tomb is included), all of which are on the Motosu side of the mountain.
Of the 290 tombs, 237 are on the Motosu side, and 162 of these have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. Both horizontal and vertical type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have been found, often with large quantities of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, which included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, iron swords and armor, horse fittings, agricultural implements, jewelry (such as round and cylindrical beads, and ''[[magatama]]''), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery, and human bones. On October 13, 2017, 687 items excavated were designated as Gifu Prefecture Important Cultural Properties and 8,093 other excavated artifacts were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Motosu city.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|qid=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
